name: HMS H 31 (N 31)
events: The history of HMS H 31 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine as well as several other files.
This page was last updated in November 2017.
2 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN) briefly conducted independent exercises of Portland. (1)
4 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kelly (Capt. L.F.A.V.N. Mountbatten, GCVO, RN) and HMS Acheron (Lt.Cdr. R.W.F. Northcott, RN). (1)
5 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Achates (Cdr. R.J. Gardner, RN). (1)
8 Sep 1939 During the night of 8/9 September 1939, HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN), conducted a night patrol off Portland. This was her 1st war patrol. (1)
11 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kelly (Capt. L.F.A.V.N. Mountbatten, GCVO, RN). (1)
12 Sep 1939 During the night of 12/13 September 1939, HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN), conducted a night patrol off Portland. This was her 2nd war patrol. (1)
16 Sep 1939 During the night of 16/17 September 1939, HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN), conducted a night patrol off Portland. This was her 3rd war patrol. (1)
17 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Fell, OBE, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Vanoc (Lt.Cdr. J.G.W. Deneys, RN) and HMS Jaguar (Lt.Cdr. J.F.W. Hine, RN). (1)
23 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kelly (Capt. L.F.A.V.N. Mountbatten, GCVO, RN) and HMS Jaguar (Lt.Cdr. J.F.W. Hine, RN).
In the evening more night gunnery exercises were carried out. (1)
25 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kingston (Lt.Cdr. P. Somerville, RN) and HMS Jaguar (Lt.Cdr. J.F.W. Hine, RN). (1)
27 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kingston (Lt.Cdr. P. Somerville, RN)

[The log of H 31 gives HMS Jaguar as the destroyer the exercises were carried out with but this can't be correct as she was no longer working up at Portland. The only destroyer there on that day was HMS Kingston.] (1)
28 Sep 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kingston (Lt.Cdr. P. Somerville, RN). (1)

14 Oct 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) shifted from Plymouth to Portland. She was escorted by HMS Maid Marion (Capt.(Retd.) D.I. McGillewie, RN). (2)
16 Oct 1939 H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted diving trials off Portsmouth as trim had to be adjusted for the heavier batteries installed. (2)

After about two hours the exercises had to be abandoned due to limited visibility. (5)

26 Jan 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) departed Portland for Sheerness. She was escorted by HMS Wolborough (Cdr.(Retd.) J.S. Hoffman, RN) and HMS Stella Sirius. (6)
27 Jan 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) arrived at the Sheerness Dockyard where she was taken in hand for refit. (6)
21 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) conducted post-refit trials off Sheerness. (7)
22 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) departed Sheerness for Portsmouth. (7)
23 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) arrived at Portsmouth. (7)
24 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (7)
25 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. These included a practice attack on HMS Warrior II (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (7)
27 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (7)
28 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (7)
29 May 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) departed Portsmouth for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the English Channel.
For the daily positions of HMS H 31 during this short patrol see the map below.
1 Jun 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) ended her 4th war patrol at Portsmouth. (8)

3 Jun 1940 The Dutch submarine HrMs O 13 (Lt.Cdr. E.H. Vorster, RNN) departed Portsmouth for Dundee. She was escorted by the British armed yacht HMS White Bear (Capt. R. Gill, RD, RNR). The British submarines HMS H 28 (Lt. E.A Woodward, RN) and HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) were also in company until Blyth. (9)
6 Jun 1940 HMS H 28 (Lt. E.A Woodward, RN) and HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) both arrive at Blyth coming from Portsmouth.
10 Jun 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. (10)
12 Jun 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. These included a practice attack on HMS White Bear (Capt. R. Gill, RD, RNR). (10)
13 Jun 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) conducted exercises off Blyth. These included a practice attack on HMS White Bear (Capt. R. Gill, RD, RNR). (10)
19 Jun 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) departed Blyth for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Sea near the north coast of The Netherlands.
For the daily positions of HMS H 31 during this patrol see the map below. Do note that the map is incomplete as there are no positions noted in the log for several days.
1 Jul 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) ended her 5th war patrol at Blyth. (8)
14 Jul 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) departed Blyth for her 6th war patrol. Again she was ordered to patrol in the North Sea near the north coast of The Netherlands.
For the daily and attack positions of HMS H 31 during this patrol see the map below.

18 Jul 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) torpedoed and sank the German auxiliary patrol vessel UJ 126 / Steiermark (422 GRT, built 1938) in the North Sea north-west of the Island of Terschelling, Netherlands in position 53°28'N, 05°01'E.
The other two trawlers were UJ 125 / Johs. Klatte and UJ 128 / Franken. Besides hunting the attacker they picked up 37 survivors. 9 crew of UJ 126 died.
0633 hours - Sighted three trawlers bearing red 135° to 160°. Own course 165°. Started attack.
0737 hours - Fired one torpedo.
0739 hours - The enemy started a hunt. Only two ships were heard hunting.
0742 hours - Depth charging started. A total of four were dropped in ten minutes. The enemy was heard hunting until around 1000 hours whan HE started to fade out.
1015 hours - No more HE heard.
1130 hours - Came to periscope depth. Nothing in sight. (8)
26 Jul 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. M.D. Wanklyn, RN) ended her 6th war patrol at Blyth. (8)
8 Aug 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) departed Blyth for her 7th war patrol. Again she was ordered to patrol off the Dutch coast.
For the daily positions of HMS H 32 during this patrol see the map below.
21 Aug 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) ended her 7th war patrol at Blyth. (8)
31 Aug 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) departed Blyth for Rothesay. She is to join the 7th Submarine Flotilla based there for training duties. Passage to Methill was made in convoy FN 267. (11)
1 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) arrived at Methill around 0915 hours. She then anchored to continue her passage to Rothesay the next day. (12)
2 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) departed Methill around for Rothesay. She made part of the passage in convoy OA 208. (12)
6 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (12)
8 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (12)
9 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Terje 3 (Skr. J.H.D. Dansie, RNR). (12)
10 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR). (12)

11 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN), HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN) and HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) (12)
12 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR). (12)
12 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (12)
14 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN). (12)
16 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) (12)
17 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN) and HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR). (12)
18 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN) and HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) (12)
19 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) (12)

21 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Terje 2 (Ch.Skr. G.R. Gale, RNR) and HMS Terje 3 (Skr. J.H.D. Dansie, RNR). Upon completion of these exercises HMS H 31 proceeded to Rothesay. (12)
25 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) conducted A/S and attack exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (12)

30 Sep 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.E. Coltart, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (12)

Norman, RN). The targets were the submarine tender HMS Cyclops (A/Capt. R.L.M. Edwards, RN) escorted by (at least) patrol yacht HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (13)

28 Nov 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) conducted attack exercises for the C.O.Q.C. (Commanding Officers Qualifying Course) in the Clyde area. (14)

21 Dec 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (15)

22 Dec 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. After having returned to harbour the engine room accidentaly flooded and the submarine sank alongside the quay.
24 Dec 1940 In the afternoon the engine room of HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) was pumped dry and the submarine was floating again.
25 Dec 1940 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) was towed from Campbeltown to Rothesay by the tug Thames. (15)
11 Jan 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) was towed from Rothesay to Greenock. (16)
12 Jan 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) was towed from Greenock to Glasgow where she was docked for repairs. (16)
20 Jan 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) was undocked. (16)
25 Jan 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) proceeded from Glasgow to Rothesay. (16)
30 Jan 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) conducted independent exercises in the Clyde area. (16)
31 Jan 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) conducted independent exercises in the Clyde area. (16)
2 Feb 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson, DSC, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area for the C.O.Q.C. (Commanding Officers Qualifying Course). (17)

16 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMCS Ottawa (Cdr. E.R. Mainguy, RCN) and ORP Garland (Lt.Cdr. K.F. Namiesniowski, ORP). (18)
17 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). (18)

18 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) conducted night exercises with HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). Upon completion of these exercises they proceeded to Rothesay where they arrived later the same day. (18)
19 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) conducted torpedo firing (salvo firing) exercises in the Clyde area. (18)
21 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) departed Rothesay for Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (18)

22 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) arrived at Tobermory. She departed later the same day togther with HMS H 33 (Lt. C.H. Rankin, RN) and HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) for Falmouth. The submarines were escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (18)
25 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) arrived at Falmouth. She departed for Plymouth later the same day. (18)
26 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) arrived at Plymouth. (18)

29 Mar 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for her 8th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay. She sailed at 0630 hours together with HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN). They were escorted until 1135 hours by HMS Lincolnshire (Lt.Cdr. W.K. Buckley, RNVR). (8)
29 Mar 1941 For the daily positions of HMS H 31 during this patrol see the map below. Do note that the map is incomplete as there are no positions noted in the log for several days.
14 Apr 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) ended her 8th war patrol and HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) ended her 11th war patrol at Falmouth.
At 0630 hours, the submarines made rendez-vous was with their escort towards Falmouth, HMS White Bear (Cdr.(Retd.) C.C. Flemming, RN). (8)

14 Apr 1941 At 0230 A/14, HMS Unbeaten (Lt. E.A. Woodward, RN) sighted a submarine in position 49°54'N, 04°45'W. This turned out to be HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) which was about 20 miles out of position, they exchanged recognition signals. (20)

16 Apr 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) and HMS H 34 (Lt. C.P. Norman, RN) both departed Falmouth for Rothesay. They were escorted by HMS White Bear (Cdr.(Retd.) C.C. Flemming, RN). (21)
18 Apr 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.D. Whiteway-Wilkinson DSC, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (21)
23 Apr 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) departed Rothesay for Londonderry. She was escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (21)
24 Apr 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) arrived at Londonderry. (21)

26 Apr 1941 In the morning, HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN), is grounded near Londonderry. Attemps were made to come off immediately but this failed. Three more attemps were made at high tide but only the third attempt (in the evening of the 27th) was successful. (21)
3 May 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle. (22)

13 Jun 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) departed Londonderry for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Northern Pride (T/Lt. A.A. Campbell, RNR). (23)
14 Jun 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (23)
21 Jun 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (23)

24 Jun 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown. (23)
24 Jun 1941 Convoy attack exercises were carried out in the Clyde area.

The convoy was made up of HMS Cyclops (A/Capt. R.L.M. Edwards, RN), which served as the 'main target'. She was escorted by HMS Alecto (Cdr. R.M.G. Gambier, RN), HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN), HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN), HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) and FFS La Moqueuse.
This convoy was attacked in the morning by two submarines; HMS Oberon (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Pizey, DSO, RN) and HMS H 43 (Lt. J.D. Martin, RN).
In the afternoon the convoy was attacked (near Campbeltown) by HMS L 27 (Lt. H.N. Edmonds, DSC, RN), HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) and HMS H 32 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN). (24)

2 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle. (26)
4 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle. (26)

10 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) departed Londonderry for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Wellard. (26)
11 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (26)
15 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) departed Rothesay for Blyth. She was escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN). (26)
18 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) and her escort, the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN), both arrived at Blyth (26)
21 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) and her escort, the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN), both departed Blyth for Sheerness. They were to proceed behind convoy FS 73. (26)
22 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) and her escort, the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN), were ordered to proceed to the Humber where they arried early in the night.
H 31 and HrMs Z 5 departed later the same day for Sheerness and were ordered to proceed astern of convoy FS 74. (26)
23 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) and her escort, the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN), arrived at Sheerness roads. (26)
24 Aug 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) shifted to the Sheerness Dockyard where she was taken in hand for refit. (26)
22 Oct 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) departed Sheerness for Rothesay. She was escorted until 2120 hours by HMS Cottesmore (Lt.Cdr. J.C.A. Ingram, RN).
At 0745/25 H 31 made rendez-vous with HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) for onwards passage to Rothesay under escort. (27)
27 Oct 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (27)
30 Oct 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) conducted torpedo firing trials in the Clyde area. (27)
1 Nov 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown from where she was to participate in A/S exercises.
[No logs are available for HMS H 31 after October 1941 so no further details then given her are currently known to us.] (28)
30 Nov 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Rothesay. (29)
3 Dec 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) departed Rothesay for Londonderry. She was escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN). (28)
4 Dec 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) arrived at Londonderry from where she was to participate in A/S exercises. (29)
15 Dec 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) departed Londonderry for Rothesay. (28)
16 Dec 1941 HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) arrived at Rothesay. She departed for her 9th war patrol after only a few hours in port. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.

Passage south through the Irish Sea was made together with HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) and HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN). They were escorted by HMS Boarhound (Skr. S.G. Jinks, RNR) until 0700/17 when HMS Alecto (Cdr. R.M.G. Gambier, RN) took over the escort until 0500/19 while off Wolf Rock. (30)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
